body: PT Bank Krom Indonesia Tbk (BBSI) reported a net profit of IDR 107.13 billion in the first nine months of 2024, up 9.54% year-on-year, driven by solid liquidity management and growth strategy. The bank's third-party funds surged 541% year-to-date to IDR 2.22 trillion, with a capital adequacy ratio of 101.38%, positioning it for future business expansion and digital innovation.
title: Krom Bank Indonesia reports 9.54% year-on-year net profit increase to IDR 107.13 billion in first nine months of 2024.

body: PT Pan Brothers Tbk (PBRX) anticipates a decline in sales in 2025 due to limited working capital, with total equity reported at USD 333.60 million in Q3 2024, and sales declining to USD 92.25 million in March 2024 from USD 110.04 million in the same period in 2023. The company plans to increase sales and stabilize working capital by 2026-2030, with potential debt reduction, and is optimistic about cash flow improvement following a restructuring proposal to be concluded by November 22, 2024.
title: PT Pan Brothers TBK Anticipates Sales Decline in 2025 Due to Limited Working Capital.

body: PT Agro Bahari Nusantara Tbk (UDNG) saw its stock performance plummet 52% at yesterday's closing bell, a year after its debut on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, where it raised IDR 50 billion in fresh funds for shrimp farm expansion. The company's transaction volume reached 217,600 shares, worth IDR 10.45 million, with a total of 1.75 billion listed shares and 1,950 shareholders, including controlling party and non-controlling parties.
title: PT Agro Bahari Nusantara's stock plummets 52% one year after debut on Indonesia Stock Exchange.

body: The Indonesian government's latest auction of state sukuk securities received IDR 16.26 trillion in incoming bids, with the highest amounts coming from the SPNS04082025 series at IDR 3.82 trillion and the PBS038 series at IDR 3.58 trillion. The total awarded amount from the 7 auctioned series was IDR 10.2 trillion, bringing the total awarded amount from 22 state sukuk auctions since the beginning of 2024 to IDR 197.82 trillion.
title: Indonesia's government raises IDR 10.2 trillion from latest state sukuk auction, with total awarded amount reaching IDR 197.82 trillion since 2024.

body: PT Adiwarna Anugerah Abadi Tbk (NAIK) has set its initial public offering (IPO) price at IDR 107 per share, the upper limit of the estimated range, and will offer 750 million shares, equivalent to 23.08% of its total issued and paid-up capital, potentially raising IDR 80.25 billion. The proceeds will be allocated towards working capital, specifically for purchasing raw materials, auxiliary materials, and consumables to support its business activities.
title: PT Adiwarna Anugerah Abadi Tbk sets IPO price at IDR 107 per share to raise up to IDR 80.25 billion.

body: PT MPX Logistics International Tbk (MPXL) will allocate IDR 1.5 billion for its interim dividend in fiscal year 2024, with a dividend payout ratio of 12.4% of its net profit of IDR 12.12 billion, which soared 57.8% year-on-year. The interim dividend per share is IDR 0.75, down 25% from the previous year, and will be disbursed on December 3, 2024, with a cumulative ex-dividend date set for November 14-15, 2024.
title: MPX Logistics to Distribute IDR 1.5 Billion Interim Dividend in December Despite 25% Decrease from Previous Year.

body: PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 5 per share, totaling IDR 316.85 billion, to its shareholders listed as of November 18, 2024, with payment scheduled for December 6, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 509.35 billion in the first nine months of 2024 and had retained earnings of IDR 7.08 trillion and total equity of IDR 8.58 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Surya Citra Media to Distribute IDR 5 Interim Dividend per Share to Shareholders.

body: PT Adi Sarana Armada Tbk (ASSA) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 20 per share, totaling IDR 73.82 billion, on November 22, 2024, following a 79.82% year-on-year increase in net profit to IDR 212.67 billion at the end of September 2024. This dividend distribution marks a significant step towards regular dividend payments, with the company reporting unrestricted retained earnings of IDR 1 billion and total equity of IDR 2.8 trillion as of September 2024.
title: PT Adi Sarana Armada to Distribute First Interim Dividend Since 2012 Amid Strong Profit Growth

body: PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk (TAPG) will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 1.51 trillion, or IDR 76 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on October 29, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 1.62 trillion in the first nine months of 2024, with unrestricted retained earnings of IDR 5.56 trillion and total equity of IDR 11.14 trillion as of September 30, 2024.
title: Triputra Agro Persada to Distribute IDR 1.51 Trillion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT MD Entertainment Tbk (MDEN) reported a net profit of IDR 102.9 billion in the first nine months of 2024, up 20.8% year-on-year, driven by a 18.6% increase in revenue to IDR 348.1 billion, supported by two blockbuster movies. The company's recent acquisition of PT Net Visi Media Tbk (NETV) will provide a free-to-air distribution channel, allowing MD Entertainment to reach a wider audience and solidify its position in the television broadcast business.
title: MD Entertainment records 20.8% net profit growth in 9M 2024, driven by blockbuster movies and acquisition of Net Visi Media.

body: PT Semen Indonesia Tbk (SMGR) reported a net profit of IDR 719.72 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 58% year-over-year decline from IDR 1.71 trillion in the same period last year. Despite a 4.9% year-over-year revenue decline to IDR 26.29 trillion, the company's cost of goods sold and selling expenses increased by 0.3% and 4.5%, respectively, contributing to the profit drop.
title: Semen Indonesia's net profit drops 58% year-on-year to IDR 719.72 billion in the first nine months of 2024.
